author: Dr. Allyson Pishko (Hematology)
content:
OUTPATIENT HEMATOLOGY FOLLOW-UP

Interval: ~2.5 weeks after discharge.

SUBJECTIVE: Doing well. Back to light activity; symptoms resolved. No bleeding, no recurrent chest pain or leg swelling. Adherent with apixaban. Anxious about the upcoming biopsy and the possibility of cancer.

OBJECTIVE: Hemodynamically normal, well-appearing. No bleeding stigmata.

ASSESSMENT/PLAN:
#Acute unprovoked VTE — clinically stable on apixaban. Given the unprovoked nature and the concurrent liver lesion, anticipate at least an extended (likely indefinite) anticoagulation course pending the malignancy workup.
#Liver mass — biopsy being scheduled with interventional radiology. We discussed that the peri-procedural anticoagulation handling will be specified by IR, and emphasized RESUMING anticoagulation PROMPTLY after the biopsy because he remains at meaningful thrombotic risk.
#Return precautions reviewed.
attestation: Electronically signed by Dr. Allyson Pishko, MD (Hematology). 7/24/2027.
